reply_content:
While I Did feel very very sorry for so so many people in Louisiana and Mississippi and I DO NOT feel anyone deserved what happened to them, I would suggest they have no idea what they are talking about, to the ones who do feel that. To return their sentiment, however, I wish they would have been one of the families who were trapped. 
I am from New Orleans. I have lived in St. Bernard. I live in St.Tammany and my family and I  frequently drive along the Mississippi Coastline. We always have. It's just part of greatest majority of residents lives.

The deaths, the devastation, the loss of hope and the inability to imagine that it could be rebuilt. Anyone who thinks another person deserved even a single day of what we endured here because "they did this to their seves, or because they chose to stay....IS A SICK P.O.S.
IMAGINE YOUR HOUSE completely submerged. For over 16 hours we listened to the wind howl where we evacuated to, my younger brother's house in Collins, MS. we watched the wind lift 1 side of our vehicles, just knowing, the next strong gust would flip it, or blow several yards away, or watch as something corrienning like a missile slammed into it. We watched 80+ year old evergreens rock back and forth with the ground pulling up in union until it softens the ground enough to finally give, leaving behind holes deep enough and wide enough to concrete into an in ground pool.
 Hundreds of pine  trees probably as far away as 2 miles snapped so fast that it sounded more like automatic weapon. 
We were either blessed, or tortured to be able to listened to WWL Radio all night. Reports of looting, police in New Orleans failing to report to work, and then eventually brief and uncertain  news of horrors happening in the Superdome. 
People jumping to their deaths, 1 or 2 women being raped, and parts of the roof pealed back exposing parts of the upper rafters whenever lightening struck close enough to see. And before I lose your interest I will gladly move on, suffice it to say those were just a couple of the things upsetting to hear within the reports as it moved northeast away from N.O. and quickly approaching our location as it literally made its way directly above, putting us directly in the eye.
That was just the beginning of what turned into worse reports of all of N.O. East and north northeast from where Lake Pontchartrain at I-10 met Slidell all now under water as far inland as possibly 3 miles. Then came reports of 2 or 3  patients at the Ochsner Hospital jumping to their death. So many more false reports were broadcast from people able to call in to the show just kept painting worsening scenarios. It was like believing that a nuclear bomb had gone off somewhere between New Orleans East and the North shore. By the time Katrina blew past us, our imaginations could not even begin to too understand what would happen moving forward. We sincerely wondered about how great a mass of death might be reported soon. That was just how August 28th ushered in the 29th. It was dreadful, windless, hot, and exhausting that morning. Then...
that lasted for 2 days. We build an out door cooking station, a shower, and of course bathroom. We gather materials for water collections, washed clothes in a river...it just got worse by the hour until some us decided to drive until we could find power. That was just the beginning of a 3 day exploration to find our way around road closures to the west side of Louisiana to try and get back into St. Tammany. 
I say all of that to tell whomever thinks some people got what they deserved that they should retract such an insensitive comment or never speak on the matter ever again.
More than 1400 people died because of Katrina. You can't imagine the smells of beginning to assess. The impossible struggles to help one another return to see what could be salvaged. That is just where the story starts. We now have 20 years of rebuilding behind us and probably as much as returned much never will. We supply the entire nation with necessities, with incredible foods, with unbelievably great entertainment and our commitment to our nation. We help not only people, but we still give an enormous amount to our countrymen in need. 
The negative comments made by fellow Americans is appalling to say the least.
